When police in Pasco County, Florida pulled over a car sporting a student driver sticker on the night of April 2nd, 2022, they likely suspected to find a new driver struggling to obey traffic laws. But, instead of a student, it was driving instructor Gregory Sorenson, and the seemingly regular traffic stop was quickly revealed to be much more serious. Throughout the encounter, Gregory would claim to be in severe pain while facing what he believed to be life-changing charges. We'll leave it up to you to draw your own conclusion as you watch the bizarre situation play out. The officer pulls Gregory Sorenson over after he notices him drifting between traffic lanes. After summoning backup, the initial officer administers several sobriety tests, including the classic challenge of asking Gregory to walk in a straight line down the side of the road.
You're not going to make it, boy.

I'm going to grab, I got to put, like I said, I'm going to walk back to your car, I'm going to grab all your stuff, and I'm going to put it in front of my car. I'm not going to leave it in your car. I'm going to grab it. All right.
So I gave you another set of cuffs.
All right. Watch yourself. Nice and easy.

As soon as he enters the car, Gregory appears to be in intense physical pain. However, his night is about to get even worse when police search his car. Gregory Sorenson was officially booked at Pasco County Jail later that night. A blood test revealed that his blood alcohol level was about 0.2, nearly three times the legal limit. It's both ironic and concerning that a driving instructor broke one of the most basic rules of the road. Thankfully, nobody was hurt as a result of his driving, but he was still charged with a DUI.
